summary: W3-K batch 2 — the two core/contract changes deferred from batch 1 of grill GRL-20260624-w3k-mcp-topology. K2 (read/write split: extract read-only fab_pending out of fab_review) and K6 (recall omitted_candidate_count → structured dropped[]{id,reason}). Both are cross-end wire-contract / shared-schema changes; planned as TWO INDEPENDENT tasks with NO cross-dependency → recommended as TWO SEPARATE PRs for isolated verification + rollback.
approach: TDD per task (RED → GREEN → REFACTOR). Each task touches packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts (zod schemas) + packages/server/src consumers + tests + (K2) the cross-end skill/policy migration surface. Mandatory ordering inside each task: edit shared schema → `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` (rebuild dist, else invalid_union_discriminator at runtime) → run server tests → `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit`. Zero-residual greps MUST use the Grep tool or node fs (bash grep is ugrep on this repo and gives false negatives on alternation). K2 and K6 share zero files in conflict beyond api-contracts.ts (different schema blocks), so they are safe to run in parallel on separate branches.
recommended_execution: Two separate PRs (one per task). Independent — no merge ordering required.

stages: K2: fab_review(8 actions) → split → fab_pending(list+search read-only) + fab_review(6 write) → migrate every caller/skill/doc/test → remove list/search from FabReview* unions

K6: producer plan-context.ts emits dropped[]{id,reason} (retrieval_budget computed once pre-trim; payload_budget appended post-trim only) → recallOutputSchema/planContextOutputSchema carry dropped[] → recall.ts consumer derives hint count from dropped.filter(reason==='retrieval_budget')

design_decisions: K2 and K6 are independent → two PRs, not one. Separate PRs give isolated verification + isolated rollback for two distinct contract risks (C-007 batch-two atomic-migration intent applies per change, not bundled).

K6 reuses the archive-scan dropped[]{key,reason} convention (C-005) verbatim shape — archive-scan uses {session_id,reason}, recall uses {id,reason}; co-locate the recall drop-reason enum near api-contracts.ts archive-scan dropped[] (~line 572) with a comment marking the shared {key,reason} convention. Zero new vocabulary invented.
K2 keeps modify-content/modify-layer explicit separation untouched (C-001) — only list+search are lifted; the 6 write actions stay in FabReview* exactly as-is.
K2: new read tool fab_pending gets readOnlyHint:true + idempotentHint:true (C-002 honest read/write split); fab_review keeps readOnlyHint:false.

patterns: MCP SDK 1.29.0 crashes on a discriminatedUnion inputSchema/outputSchema → every tool keeps a FLATTENED *Shape (ZodRawShape) alongside its discriminatedUnion *Schema (see FabReviewInputShape/FabReviewOutputSchema rationale comments). fab_pending MUST follow the same Shape+Schema pair pattern.

Annotations object literal per tool (readOnlyHint/idempotentHint) — read tools (recall:248, archive-scan:393, others:528/587) use readOnlyHint:true,idempotentHint:true; fabReviewAnnotations:1255 uses false/false.
Producer-consumer round-trip: plan-context service produces the field, recall service consumes it (services/recall.ts:210). Both ends migrate together or the union discriminator / consumer breaks.
conventions: ESM, strict TypeScript, zod schemas in packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts
After ANY api-contracts.ts edit: `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` BEFORE running server tests (rebuild dist, avoid invalid_union_discriminator).
Zero-residual checks via Grep tool / node fs, NEVER bash grep (ugrep false negatives on this repo).
tsc gate: `pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit` must be 0 before push (local tsup --dts ≠ CI tsc --noEmit).
